examples: Pluripotent cells can generate any cell type, but not an entire organism.
A way around the problem is something called an induced pluripotent stem cell, or iPS.
These so-called induced pluripotent stem cells, or IPS cells, could now become any cell.
The technique takes the same sample of skin cells but converts them using proteins to "induced pluripotent" stem cells.
A. A stem cell is a pluripotent cell, meaning a cell that can develop into any kind of tissue.
